title: Adobe Sign Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Adobe Acrobat Sign** is Adobe's e-signature platform exposing a REST API at shard-specific origins (`https://api.na1.adobesign.com`, `https://api.eu1.adobesign.com`, `https://api.jp1.adobesign.com`, etc.). This connector — `AdobeSignConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "adobe_sign"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "oauth2_code"`) — wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs from an Adobe Sign account:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Base URI discovery | `/api/rest/v6/baseUris` | Per-user shard discovery — MUST run once after token exchange |
| Agreements | `/api/rest/v6/agreements` | create, list, get, remind, cancel, download |
| Library documents | `/api/rest/v6/libraryDocuments` | list + read reusable templates |
| Users | `/api/rest/v6/users` | list + read account users |
| Workflows | `/api/rest/v6/workflows` | list published custom workflows |
| Webhooks | `/api/rest/v6/webhooks` | list + create webhook subscriptions |

The connector normalises agreements + library documents into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{connector_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff (3 attempts), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC — all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::AdobeSignHTTPClient`).

### Shard discovery — the Adobe Sign gotcha

Adobe Sign accounts live on a specific shard (data centre). After exchanging the OAuth code at the OAuth host (`https://secure.na1.adobesign.com`), the resulting access token is ONLY valid against the user's home shard. The connector calls `GET /api/rest/v6/baseUris` immediately after token exchange, reads `apiAccessPoint` from the response, and pivots its API base URL onto the right shard. Presenting a token to the wrong shard returns 401 `INVALID_API_ACCESS_POINT`.

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Create an Adobe Sign integration

1. Sign in to https://console.adobe.io.
2. Create a new project → **Add API → Adobe Sign**.
3. Choose **OAuth Web App**. Configure your **Redirect URI** to match what the Shielva gateway uses (typically `https://gateway.example.com/oauth/callback/adobe_sign`).
4. Grant the **scopes** the connector needs (default: `user_read`, `agreement_read`, `agreement_write`, `agreement_send`, `library_read`, `library_write`, `workflow_read`).
5. Copy the **Client ID** and **Client Secret**.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → Adobe Sign**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `client_id` (text) | OAuth client ID from step 5 |
| `client_secret` (secret) | OAuth client secret from step 5 |
| `oauth_host` (text, optional) | Defaults to `https://secure.na1.adobesign.com` — works for all shards because shard discovery runs post-exchange |
| `scopes` (text, optional) | Space-delimited scope string; must match what the integration grants in Adobe Developer Console |
| `timeout_s` (number, optional) | Default 60s |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `client_id` + `client_secret` are present (it does NOT call the API), initialises the `AdobeSignHTTPClient`, and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, PENDING)`.

### 3. Complete OAuth

The ACP gateway redirects the operator through the Adobe Sign authorize URL (`{oauth_host}/public/oauth/v2?response_type=code&client_id=...&scope=...&redirect_uri=...&state=...`). On success Adobe redirects back with `?code=<auth_code>`. The gateway calls `connector.authorize(auth_code=...)`, which:

1. `POST {oauth_host}/oauth/v2/token` with `grant_type=authorization_code`.
2. `GET /baseUris` against the new access token to discover the shard.
3. Pivots `api_base_url` onto `{apiAccessPoint}api/rest/v6` and persists it.

### 4. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which hits `GET /users/me` as a lightweight probe. 2xx → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. 401 → `OFFLINE + TOKEN_EXPIRED`. 429 → `DEGRADED + CONNECTED` with a structured-log warning.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Adobe Sign REST API v6 uses **OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code Grant** (`AUTH_TYPE = "oauth2_code"`).

### Credential model

- `client_id` + `client_secret` — Adobe Developer Console integration credentials. Required at install.
- `access_token` — Bearer token issued via `/oauth/v2/token`. Stored in config post-authorize. Sent as `Authorization: Bearer <token>` on every API call.
- `refresh_token` — long-lived token for `/oauth/v2/refresh`. Stored in token info.
- `api_access_point` — shard-discovered API origin (e.g. `https://api.eu1.adobesign.com/`). Persisted post-authorize so subsequent restarts hit the right shard.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `client_id` + `client_secret`. Initialises `AdobeSignHTTPClient`. Does NOT call Adobe. |
| `get_oauth_url(redirect_uri, state)` | Builds `{oauth_host}/public/oauth/v2?response_type=code&client_id=...&scope=...&redirect_uri=...&state=...`. |
| `authorize(auth_code, state)` | `POST /oauth/v2/token` then `GET /baseUris` (shard discovery) — persists `api_access_point`. |
| `health_check()` | `GET /users/me`. |

### Header contract

Every API request to `{api_base_url}/*`:

```
Authorization: Bearer <access_token>
Accept:        application/json
Content-Type:  application/json   (when sending a body)
```

OAuth token endpoint requests use `application/x-www-form-urlencoded`.

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `client_id` | text | yes | `self.client_id` | OAuth client ID |
| `client_secret` | secret | yes | `self.client_secret` | OAuth client secret |
| `oauth_host` | text | no (default `https://secure.na1.adobesign.com`) | `self.oauth_host` | OAuth authorize + token endpoints |
| `api_access_point` | text | no — discovered post-authorize | `self.api_access_point` | Shard-specific API origin |
| `api_base_url` | text | no — derived from `api_access_point` | `self.api_base_url` | Effective `{origin}/api/rest/v6` |
| `scopes` | text | no (default scope bundle) | `self.scopes` | OAuth scope string |
| `timeout_s` | number | no (default 30) | `self.timeout_s` | Per-request httpx timeout |
| `access_token` | secret | no — populated post-authorize | `self.access_token` | Bearer token |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["client_id", "client_secret"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE",   "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
_DEFAULT_OAUTH_HOST = "https://secure.na1.adobesign.com"
_DEFAULT_API_BASE   = "https://api.na1.adobesign.com/api/rest/v6"
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `AdobeSignConnector`. SOC: `connector.py` orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::AdobeSignHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `client_id` + `client_secret`. Does NOT call Adobe.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
Exchange the OAuth code at `{oauth_host}/oauth/v2/token`, then call `/baseUris` and pivot the API base URL onto the discovered shard.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
`GET /users/me`. Classifies failures by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Iterates agreements + library documents → `NormalizedDocument` → `ingest_document`. Returns `SyncResult` with `documents_synced / _failed / _found`.

### `async get_base_uris() -> Dict`
`GET /baseUris` — returns `{apiAccessPoint, webAccessPoint}` for shard discovery.

children.title: Agreements
children.content:
### `async create_agreement(payload) -> Dict`
`POST /agreements`. Body shape:
```json
{
  "fileInfos": [{"libraryDocumentId": "..."} | {"transientDocumentId": "..."}],
  "participantSetsInfo": [
    {"memberInfos": [{"email": "signer@ex.com"}], "order": 1, "role": "SIGNER"}
  ],
  "name": "NDA — Acme",
  "signatureType": "ESIGN",
  "state": "IN_PROCESS"
}
```

### `async get_agreement(agreement_id) -> Dict`
`GET /agreements/{agreementId}`.

### `async list_agreements(*, cursor=None, page_size=100) -> Dict`
`GET /agreements?pageSize=...&cursor=...`. Cursor-paginated; `nextCursor` lives in the response.

### `async send_reminder(agreement_id, participant_emails=None, note=None) -> Dict`
`POST /agreements/{agreementId}/reminders`. Body:
```json
{"recipientParticipantIds": ["pid-1"], "status": "ACTIVE", "note": "..."}
```
Adobe expects participant IDs, not emails — resolve them first via `get_agreement(...)['participantSetsInfo']`.

### `async cancel_agreement(agreement_id, comment=None, notify_signer=True) -> Dict`
`PUT /agreements/{agreementId}/state`. Body:
```json
{"state": "CANCELLED", "agreementCancellationInfo": {"comment": "...", "notifyOthers": true}}
```

### `async download_agreement(agreement_id) -> bytes`
`GET /agreements/{agreementId}/combinedDocument`. Returns the signed PDF as raw bytes.

children.title: Library Documents
children.content:
### `async list_library_documents(*, cursor=None, page_size=100) -> Dict`
`GET /libraryDocuments`.

### `async get_library_document(library_document_id) -> Dict`
`GET /libraryDocuments/{libraryDocumentId}`.

children.title: Users + Workflows
children.content:
### `async list_users(*, cursor=None) -> Dict`
`GET /users`.

### `async get_user(user_id) -> Dict`
`GET /users/{userId}`.

### `async list_workflows(*, cursor=None) -> Dict`
`GET /workflows` — list published custom workflows.

children.title: Webhooks
children.content:
### `async list_webhooks(*, cursor=None) -> Dict`
`GET /webhooks`.

### `async create_webhook(payload) -> Dict`
`POST /webhooks`. Body:
```json
{
  "name": "MyHook",
  "scope": "ACCOUNT",
  "state": "ACTIVE",
  "webhookSubscriptionEvents": ["AGREEMENT_ALL"],
  "webhookUrlInfo": {"url": "https://gateway.example.com/hook"}
}
```

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
AdobeSignError                       # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── AdobeSignAuthError                # 401 / 403 (incl. INVALID_API_ACCESS_POINT)
├── AdobeSignBadRequestError          # 400 (Adobe `code` field — INVALID_PARAMETER, etc.)
├── AdobeSignNotFoundError            # 404
├── AdobeSignConflictError            # 409 (state conflict — e.g. cancel-already-cancelled)
├── AdobeSignRateLimitError           # 429 — retry_after_s
└── AdobeSignServerError              # 5xx + transport errors
```

Back-compat aliases preserved:
```
AdobeSignNetworkError = AdobeSignServerError
AdobeSignNotFound     = AdobeSignNotFoundError
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 / 409 | Raise immediately |
| 429 | Exponential backoff `_BACKOFF_BASE * 2 ** attempt` (0.5s, 1s, 2s), retry up to `_MAX_RETRIES=3`. `Retry-After` header honoured when present. |
| 5xx | Same exponential backoff, retry up to 3 |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` / `httpx.NetworkError` | Backoff, retry up to 3, then raise `AdobeSignServerError` |

### Shard mismatch (`INVALID_API_ACCESS_POINT`)

If Adobe returns 401 with `code: INVALID_API_ACCESS_POINT`, the token is valid but presented to the wrong shard. Re-run `authorize()` (or explicitly `get_base_uris()` + update `api_base_url`) to fix.

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, TOKEN_EXPIRED)
403 → ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
429 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED) — logged as warning
5xx → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE,  CONNECTED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `client_id` or `client_secret` blank | Re-enter both in ACP. |
| OAuth popup shows `invalid_scope` | Scopes in `scopes` field don't match the Adobe Developer Console integration | Edit the integration in Adobe Developer Console — grant the same scopes — or trim the `scopes` field to the subset Adobe granted. |
| `AdobeSignAuthError` with `INVALID_API_ACCESS_POINT` | Token presented to wrong shard | Re-run `authorize()` — shard discovery will pivot `api_base_url`. If the account moved between shards, ACP must clear `api_access_point` and re-authorize. |
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` at health_check | 401 — access token expired | Refresh via `refresh_access_token` in the HTTP client (gateway-orchestrated). If refresh fails, re-authorize. |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 403 — token lacks scope (most commonly `user_read`) | Edit the integration in Adobe Developer Console to grant the missing scope, then re-authorize. |
| `adobe_sign.http.retry` warnings spike | 429 — Adobe rate limit hit | The client retries with exponential backoff. Reduce concurrent calls or batch operations. |
| `send_reminder` returns 400 `INVALID_PARAMETER` | Passed emails instead of participant IDs | Resolve `participantSetsInfo[*].memberInfos[*].id` via `get_agreement(...)` first, then pass those IDs. |
| `cancel_agreement` returns 409 `INVALID_AGREEMENT_STATE` | Agreement already cancelled or already signed | Read `status` via `get_agreement(...)` and only cancel when status is `OUT_FOR_SIGNATURE`. |
| `ImportError: cannot import name 'AdobeSignNetworkError'` | Older `exceptions.py` without back-compat aliases | Already fixed — `AdobeSignNetworkError = AdobeSignServerError` alias added in `exceptions.py`. |
